pi_items.source: Multiple authoritative sources confirm GCompris stores the database in the .config directory, not .local/share. The setup_gcompris.sh script explicitly creates /home/ga/.config/gcompris/ directory. Web searches and documentation consistently show the default database location is ~/.config/gcompris/gcompris_sqlite.db (see: https://manpages.debian.org/testing/gcompris/gcompris.6.en.html, https://gcompris.net/faq-en.html)
This is a task parameter specified in task.json description and used consistently throughout setup_task.sh and export_result.sh. The task is designed to create a user with this specific first name.
This is a task parameter specified in task.json description and used consistently throughout setup_task.sh and export_result.sh. The task is designed to create a user with this specific last name.
This is a task parameter specified in task.json description and used consistently throughout setup_task.sh and export_result.sh. The task is designed to create a user with birth year 2018.

privileged_info_summary: The create_student_profile task requires creating a new GCompris user with first name 'Alex', last name 'Miller', and birth year 2018. However, the database glob pattern in task metadata is contradicted: task.json specifies '/home/ga/.local/share/GCompris/GCompris-*.db', but GCompris standard locations (confirmed through official documentation, Debian manpages, and environment setup scripts) place the database at '~/.config/gcompris/gcompris_sqlite.db'. The target user profile fields (firstname='Alex', lastname='Miller', birthyear=2018) are verified as the intended task parameters. The data is not synthetic—it represents real GCompris database records created during task execution via the UI.
